description_long: This object is a vintage, box-shaped radio receiver with a wooden exterior and a flat, rectangular top surface. The top panel is a darker brown, possibly made of a different wood or covered with a veneer, and features a series of small round holes arranged in a line, likely serving as a speaker grille. There is a thin, metallic antenna protruding from the top, as well as a strip of white tape or label affixed to the surface. The sides and ends of the box are lighter in color, with visible wood grain and some signs of wear or damage, such as a chipped corner. The overall construction appears utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. The object is compact and portable, suitable for placement on a table or shelf in an urban environment.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden radio receiver with a dark top panel featuring a speaker grille, a protruding antenna, and a white label. The sides are light wood, showing some wear and utilitarian construction.
description_view_0: The top and one long side are visible, showing the dark brown top with holes, a protruding antenna, and a white label; the light wood side is plain.
description_view_1: The end view highlights the white label on the top and the alignment of the speaker holes, with the light wood end panel visible.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is shown, with the dark top, antenna, and label visible; the side appears more angular and less worn.
description_view_3: The other end view shows the white label more prominently and the antenna extending from the top, with the end panel in light wood.
